Notes: Total solar eclipse visible in Luxor, Jeddah, Mecca, Sana'a, and Tunisia. Maximum duration of totality observed in Egypt, approximately 37 miles southeast of Luxor, lasting 6 minutes and 22 seconds. First of three total solar eclipses observable in Tunisia in the 21st century.
Path of Totality: Eastern Atlantic Ocean, Strait of Gibraltar, Spain, Morocco, North Africa, Middle East, Horn of Africa
Cities in Total Phase: Luxor (Egypt), Jeddah, Mecca (Saudi Arabia), Sana'a (Yemen), Tunisia

Notes: Total solar eclipse visible in various countries across Europe and Central Asia. Part of Saros cycle 155, with a total of 71 events. Longest total eclipses to occur on October 26, 2144, and November 7, 2162, lasting 4 minutes and 5 seconds.
Path of Totality: Europe, Central Asia, Norway, Sweden, Finland, Estonia, European part of Russia, Kazakhstan, Uzbekistan, Kyrgyzstan, Tajikistan, China
Cities in Total Phase: Umeå, Tampere, Helsinki, Narva, Saint Petersburg, Tver, Ryazan, Saratov, Atyrau, Tashkent, Kashgar
